session.objective: Issue #1782: stop pr_description.py from silently dropping change claims inside human-authored <details> blocks. Replace unconditional <details> strip with bot-summary allowlist (chore(deps), fix(deps), renovate, dependabot, bump). Cover bot-strip, human-preserve, and no-summary-preserve in tests.

workLog.action: Selected issue #1782 from candidates JSON. Branch name feat/1782-autonomous + clear acceptance criteria + bounded change in scripts/validation/pr_description.py made it the highest-ROI single-PR fit. Verified no existing PR for the branch.
workLog.evidence: candidates-rjmurillo__ai-agents.json contained 45 entries; gh pr list --head feat/1782-autonomous returned [].

workLog.action: Read scripts/validation/pr_description.py and confirmed _strip_informational_sections strips every <details>...</details> block unconditionally (line ~155 pre-fix). Read tests/test_validation_pr_description.py to map existing coverage.
workLog.evidence: Existing test test_strips_details_blocks encoded the buggy behavior (block with no summary stripped). test_details_blocks_ignored covered the chore(deps) bot path.

workLog.action: Implemented the bot-summary allowlist: added _BOT_DETAILS_SUMMARY_PATTERN matching chore(deps)|fix(deps)|renovate|dependabot|^bump (case-insensitive) and a new _strip_bot_details_blocks helper. Routed _strip_informational_sections through it so the default behavior preserves <details> blocks.
workLog.evidence: Diff in scripts/validation/pr_description.py: +44/-3 lines. Behavior change: bot summaries -> stripped; human summaries -> preserved; no <summary> -> preserved (conservative).

workLog.action: Updated tests/test_validation_pr_description.py. Replaced test_strips_details_blocks (which encoded the bug) with three tests: test_preserves_details_block_without_summary, test_strips_renovate_details_block, test_strips_dependabot_details_block, and test_preserves_human_details_block_with_file_claims. Added an end-to-end test_human_details_block_surfaces_file_claims through extract_mentioned_files.
workLog.evidence: Test file diff: +66/-7 lines. All five new tests cover positive/negative/edge per AGENTS.md TESTING-RIGOR.

workLog.action: Validated. python3.14 -m pytest tests/test_pr_description.py tests/test_validation_pr_description.py -> 91 passed. python3.14 -m ruff check on the two touched files -> All checks passed. ruff format would reformat both files, but the diff is entirely pre-existing code on main (verified by stashing and re-running), so no unrelated reformat was applied per the no-refactor constraint.
workLog.evidence: pytest output: 91 passed in 1.47s. ruff check exit 0. git stash + ruff format --check on stashed state reproduced the same 'Would reformat' output before any of this session's edits were present.

workLog.action: Committed, pushed, and opened a PR against main with Fixes #1782. Enabled auto-merge so required checks gate the merge.
workLog.evidence: See endingCommit and the PR URL printed at the end of the session.

nextSteps: If review identifies additional bot generators (e.g. release-please summaries), extend _BOT_DETAILS_SUMMARY_PATTERN.
Consider documenting in CONTRIBUTING.md that <details> blocks now carry change claims unless the summary matches a known bot prefix (deferred per acceptance criterion 'if relevant').
